Package space.sunqian.fs.utils.jdbc

Class SimpleJdbcPool.Builder

java.lang.Object
space.sunqian.fs.utils.jdbc.SimpleJdbcPool.Builder
Enclosing interface:
SimpleJdbcPool
public static class SimpleJdbcPool.Builder extends Object
Builder class for SimpleJdbcPool.

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool
    build()
    Builds and returns a new SimpleJdbcPool.
    @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder
    closer(@Nonnull Consumer<@Nonnull Connection> closer)
    Sets the closer for closing database connections.
    @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder
    connectionFactory(@Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.ConnectionFactory connectionFactory)
    Sets the connection factory for creating underlying database connections.
    @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder
    connectionWrapperFactory(@Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.ConnectionWrapperFactory connectionWrapperFactory)
    Sets the connection wrapper factory for wrapping underlying database connections.
    @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder
    coreSize(int coreSize)
    Sets the core size of the connection pool.
    @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder
    driverClassName(@Nullable String driver)
    Sets the JDBC driver class name.
    @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder
    idleTimeout(@Nonnull Duration idleTimeout)
    Sets the idle timeout for connections in the pool.
    @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder
    maxSize(int maxSize)
    Sets the maximum size of the connection pool.
    @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder
    password(@Nullable String password)
    Sets the password for the database connection, default is null.
    @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder
    url(@Nonnull String url)
    Sets the JDBC URL for the database connection.
    @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder
    username(@Nullable String username)
    Sets the username for the database connection, default is null.
    @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder
    validator(@Nonnull Predicate<@Nonnull Connection> validator)
    Sets the validator for checking connection validity.

  • Constructor Details

    • Builder

      public Builder()

  • Method Details

    • url

      @Nonnull public @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder url(@Nonnull @Nonnull String url)
      Sets the JDBC URL for the database connection.
      Parameters:
      url - the JDBC URL
      Returns:
      this builder

    • username

      @Nonnull public @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder username(@Nullable @Nullable String username)
      Sets the username for the database connection, default is null.
      Parameters:
      username - the username
      Returns:
      this builder

    • password

      @Nonnull public @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder password(@Nullable @Nullable String password)
      Sets the password for the database connection, default is null.
      Parameters:
      password - the password
      Returns:
      this builder

    • driverClassName

      @Nonnull public @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder driverClassName(@Nullable @Nullable String driver)
      Sets the JDBC driver class name.
      Parameters:
      driver - the JDBC driver class name
      Returns:
      this builder

    • coreSize

      @Nonnull public @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder coreSize(int coreSize) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Sets the core size of the connection pool. This is the minimum number of connections that will be maintained in the pool. Default is 5.
      Parameters:
      coreSize - the core size of the connection pool
      Returns:
      this builder
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if coreSize <= 0

    • maxSize

      @Nonnull public @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder maxSize(int maxSize) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Sets the maximum size of the connection pool. This is the maximum number of connections that can be created in the pool. Default is 10.
      Parameters:
      maxSize - the maximum size of the connection pool
      Returns:
      this builder
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if maxSize < coreSize

    • idleTimeout

      @Nonnull public @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder idleTimeout(@Nonnull @Nonnull Duration idleTimeout) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Sets the idle timeout for connections in the pool. Connections that have been idle for longer than this duration will be eligible for removal during cleanup. Default is 5 minutes.
      Parameters:
      idleTimeout - the idle timeout for connections
      Returns:
      this builder
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if idleTimeout <= 0

    • connectionFactory

      @Nonnull public @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder connectionFactory(@Nonnull @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.ConnectionFactory connectionFactory)
      Sets the connection factory for creating underlying database connections. By default, connections are created using 
      
 DriverManager.getConnection(url, username, password);
      .
      Parameters:
      connectionFactory - the connection factory for creating underlying database connections
      Returns:
      this builder

    • connectionWrapperFactory

      @Nonnull public @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder connectionWrapperFactory(@Nonnull @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.ConnectionWrapperFactory connectionWrapperFactory)
      Sets the connection wrapper factory for wrapping underlying database connections. By default, connections are wrapped with pooled behavior.
      Parameters:
      connectionWrapperFactory - the connection wrapper factory for wrapping underlying database connections
      Returns:
      this builder

    • closer

      @Nonnull public @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder closer(@Nonnull @Nonnull Consumer<@Nonnull Connection> closer)
      Sets the closer for closing database connections. By default, connections are closed using 
      
 connection.close();
      .
      Parameters:
      closer - the closer for closing database connections
      Returns:
      this builder

    • validator

      @Nonnull public @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool.Builder validator(@Nonnull @Nonnull Predicate<@Nonnull Connection> validator)
      Sets the validator for checking connection validity. By default, connections are validated if 
      
 connection.isValid(1);
      .
      Parameters:
      validator - the validator for checking connection validity
      Returns:
      this builder

    • build

      @Nonnull public @Nonnull SimpleJdbcPool build() throws IllegalArgumentException, SqlRuntimeException
      Builds and returns a new SimpleJdbcPool.
      Returns:
      the built new SimpleJdbcPool
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if some configuration is invalid
      SqlRuntimeException - if failed to build the pool
